item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love scps like this! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here, I guess?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My face lit up with uncontrollable joy when I saw this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can I just say how incredible it is see someone make Where's Wally absolutely terrifying? A great example of silly on paper but amazing in terms of execution.

Definitely gets a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hate to sound like everyone else here, but this is great. Enjoy the +1.
We may not have found Waldo, but Waldo sure as hell found you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how complicated and sort of hard to follow the containment procedures were, which later clicked perfectly into place after reading the description. Pretty ingenious of the Foundation to devise that system before they were all killed :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP, it´s trully ingenious.

I´d only like to point out that I´m confused on one thing about his appearance. Does he have eyeholes but no eyeballs? Or he jusy has no eyes, like nothing at all.

If the first one´s okay, I´d recommend to change it to "lack of eyeballs".

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Hey thanks for using my drawing, I love SCPS that are based off popular brands or characters with a cool/ creepy twist to it.

item.title: Re: someone else's idea
item.content:encoded: This is now canon.

item.title: Re: someone else's idea
item.content:encoded: oh my god i love that idea

item.title: someone else's idea
item.content:encoded:
as per Splicer on SomethingAwful:

Grimdark murder monster shoving itself out of people and also is Wally for some reason -> ugh
Grimdark murder monster shoving itself out of people because it really loves where's Wally and thinks it's playing a fun game of hide and seek -> aaaaaaaaaaaaaa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus fuck. This is a spooky-scary-murder-monster skip, but done horribly right.
Take your +1, you madman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll fix M-2'a dialogue, but they're just teasing.

I did not pick up on that *at all*, sad to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For example, I have re-read this sentence so many times, and I am no closer to getting at what it's saying

Will fix.

The dialog during the house-check could also use a bit of spit-n-shine, I think:

I'll fix M-2'a dialogue, but they're just teasing. At a bad time, mind you, but they don't hate each other.

Also also also, how can a computer translator know if its memetic? Context/location/visual identity/etc

The computer isn't determining that, M-2 was, with their training and all that.

Thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great skip, don't get me wrong, but all the stuff in the containment procedures about the chambers and the self-driving vehicles and also some trucks and "the system" and water treatment rooms and… was needlessly confusing and hard to read, and I honestly could not repeat back the sequence of events required. And, not going to lie, I still don't know what "the system" is even referring to.

+ show block
– hide block

There is a location in which said vehicles are inserted into the system without interacting with any of the Containment Chambers.

For example, I have re-read this sentence so many times, and I am no closer to getting at what it's saying.

The dialog during the house-check could also use a bit of spit-n-shine, I think:

M-1: Transcript number… 67. We are entering the house.

M-3: No one's talking to you Melly.

M-2: Stop that. Are you forgetting that there's an unknown anomaly in this house? Stay focused.

This back and forth struck me as oddly out of place and needlessly antagonistic. Having a team composed of people that honestly don't like each other and it showing through their speech is a possibly interesting dynamic, but here, it doesn't lead anywhere or end up going past this.

M-2: I have discovered the object. It was rested on the toilet.

This is just bad English.

M-2: It's most likely memetic. Here. let me translate it. I have training.

M-2 takes out a small Foundation made translator, and inputs the phrase.

M-2: It doesn't appear to be memetic.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

Another odd sequence, here. M-2 doesn't sound like a normal person talking, it's very robotic. Also, going from "It's memetic!" to "Oh, guess not." is a weird swerve that doesn't have a payoff, and "having training" meaning "just plugs phrase into program" is a disconnect, too. (Also, 'let' needs to be capitalized or turn the period into a comma. Also also, it should be 'Foundation-made'. Also also also, how can a computer translator know if its memetic? Context/location/visual identity/etc. could all theoretically add to/create/influence memetic effects outside of what the actual words are.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: please run this through a few more drafts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think that most of the upvotes from this article are coming from a place of "lol waldo scp" and like sometimes an article like that is fine, but you already did it with the "lol minecraft scp" one. the containment procedures are good for something that nobody is allowed to no the location of, but that anomalous property deserves to be on something much better than "lmao it's waldo". (-1 in case that wasn't clear)
Waldo is happy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, i can see why people had a problem w/ the first image. hopefully this makes the image more tolerable and less dumb looking lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't think that somebody could ever make Where's Waldo scary, but I clearly stand corrected. The note at the end was a nice touch - I was wondering how the Foundation could've possibly contained it if they couldn't know where it is. This line especially sealed the deal for me:

No need to pray for me. Instead, pray that you never find Waldo.

Easy +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stating here real quick that pastarasta1 has created a new image, and I have replaced the old image with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's like a creepy mix of 106, 096, and 2521. definitely a very disturbing and scary infohazard, especially since the foundation can't even know if it's contained or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding Roget re:the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Im going to have to continue to think about this one. For now, I am a no-vote. The body of the work is sufficiently creepy and worthy of the main list, but the ConProcs were a major slog for me to get through and I had to reread a few parts (some more than once) in order to make sure I understood what was going on. That's honestly just as much a commentary on me as it is the article so there's only so much I can hold against you in that regard.

You succeeded in doing something relatively new in a way I've not really seen tried before, so, I have to salute that on its own. When you add the fact the rest of the piece is altogether well-executed, I gotta salute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this, the image looked kinda off and I feel the article wouldn't be impacted negatively if it wasn't there. I also agree with rattles and aismallard, tone could use a little bit of work.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i could definitely redraw it. if enough people have problems with the drawing I’ll redraw it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks fer readin

+ show block
– hide block

It's not very like the Foundation to tag on these sorts of warnings. There are the RAISA meta-notices regarding article content, but this is the kind of thing which should just be in the conprocs. I'm sort of willing to let it slide since it's for a hook, but I personally would've just made a more clinical variant of this the first sentence of the containment procedures.

That's fair I suppose. It was meant, like you said, as a hook, and also an explanation on why there's quite a few [LOCATION REDACTED]s all over the place, but I suppose that would be self explanatory. Will think about this.

It seems to me this designation is exclusive to SCP-4885.1 I don't think they would use a "generic" designation for an anomaly-specific chamber type.

*shrug* I feel like that's a stylistic choice, I'll see what I can do.

This part is a bit confusing to me. How exactly did they implant a GPS tracker onto SCP-4885? Why is the information just directly available to personnel instead of being directly fed into whatever system displays the location on the containment chamber monitors?

"How exactly did they implant a GPS tracker onto SCP-4885"

With a couple of D-Class I presume.

"Why is the information just directly available to personnel instead of being directly fed into whatever system displays the location on the containment chamber monitors?"

The information is basically unavailable to personnel. There are only like 2 situations where someone would have to access it, and they'd have to undergo training in order to access it safely. Also, it is directly linked to the containment chamber monitors.

I don't really see this footnote as necessary, you could achieve the same effect by just saying [LOCATION EXPUNGED] or something.

Yeah, that makes more sense.

Saying they're trained and then pulling out their phone is a bit weird. Also I really don't think the Foundation would allow that sort of use of third-party technology, they would just have a portable AIC unit or something perform translations for them.

That would make more sense, but the google translate thing came from me reading an article a long while back where there was a translator in the middle east that saw a warning on a wall warning people for mines, and he couldn't figure out a few of the words so he pulled out google translate lol. Now that you mention it, it would make some sense for a special device to do it, yeah.

This could just be a regular addendum imo.

Fair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I greatly enjoy scips with interesting or clever containment procedures, so I was rather engaged by this article, and the horror elements were portrayed well. Also first time I've seen pastarasta art in a mainlister.

However, I largely agree with rattles' criticisms. The tone is not as strong as it could be for many parts of the article.

+ Nitpicks
- Nitpicks

No information regarding any specific location on Earth is to exist on SCP-4885's file unless given direct permission by the O5 Council.

It's not very like the Foundation to tag on these sorts of warnings. There are the RAISA meta-notices regarding article content, but this is the kind of thing which should just be in the conprocs. I'm sort of willing to let it slide since it's for a hook, but I personally would've just made a more clinical variant of this the first sentence of the containment procedures.

Class-17 Containment Chamber

It seems to me this designation is exclusive to SCP-4885.1 I don't think they would use a "generic" designation for an anomaly-specific chamber type.

Thirty six Class-17 Containment Chambers are to be connected to a rail circuit which connects a specific vehicle between each chamber.

"connects a specific vehicle between each chamber" is confusingly worded. Why not just say each chamber is attached to an independent self-driving vehicle?

216 dummy Containment Chambers

It is not clear to me what "dummy" means in this context. If they're empty and available for containment, they should be called that. If they're fake, it should be clarified why having a fake containment chamber assists in containment of the scip.

One D-Class personnel

It's a bit awkward to use "personnel" in a specifically singular context. I would say "subject".

A "random number generator" is to choose a random number between one and thirty-six,

There is no reason for this to be in quotes. Also instead of repeating a magic number, I would just say that it randomly selects a destination. Finally, all this stuff about the algorithm being unknown is superfluous, just describe it as a cryptographically-secure pseudorandom number generator (CSPRNG) and be done with it. They are specifically designed to be unguessable.

This message consists of the current location of SCP-4885, delivered via an implanted tracking device. The GPS is only accessible by Level 5 Personnel or SCP-4885 containment staff on a need to know basis.

This part is a bit confusing to me. How exactly did they implant a GPS tracker onto SCP-4885? Why is the information just directly available to personnel instead of being directly fed into whatever system displays the location on the containment chamber monitors?

and that attempted access is punishable by immediate amnestication and loss of job as decided by the Ethics Committee.

"loss of job" -> "loss of employment"

SCP-4885 will move to the nearest wall and begin to "phase" into it.

"SCP-4885 will move through the nearest wall as if they were incorporeal" or something, using "phase" in quotes in nonclinical imo.

[REDACTED]2

I don't really see this footnote as necessary, you could achieve the same effect by just saying [LOCATION EXPUNGED] or something.

M-2: It's most likely memetic. Here. let me translate it. I have training.

M-2 takes out their phone and opens Google Translate, and inputs the phrase.

Saying they're trained and then pulling out their phone is a bit weird. Also I really don't think the Foundation would allow that sort of use of third-party technology, they would just have a portable AIC unit or something perform translations for them.

Following SCP-4885's theorized successful containment, this document has been declassified

This could just be a regular addendum imo.

I really like the concept and some of the ideas here, but for now this is a neutralvote.

Footnotes
1. Given the procedures dictate that "No personnel who are aware of SCP-4885's existence are to go within 100 meters of any Class-17 Containment Chamber.".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say that the picture really took me out of the article. It looks really bad and not unnerving or scary in the least.

item.title: Westrin you absolute genius
item.content:encoded:
It's not often that I find a reference-based scp that flows well. I also really like the MTF log.

+1
I was intrigued after reading the conprocs, then I got the description and thought goddamnit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the suggestions, but if I may add something

In the event that Location i's whereabouts are discovered at large, the next personnel located within Document PAINT-THE-TOWN-YELLOW will establish the next Location i, and their immediate family will be notified of their death.

This is not a word choice gaffe, but it's worth nothing that the rest of the file doesn't explain what this document is.

This is an intentional stylistic choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite good in concept and execution, but I honestly think it still has tons of phrasing and word choice gaffes that ought to have been rooted out in crit. In a lot of places, this article falls into the trap of using complex language to sound clinical where simpler language would suffice.

Some Examples
Some Examples

Currently, it is only theorized that SCP-4885 is contained. To further elaborate, SCP-4885 is contained within an unknown Class-17 Containment Chamber in an unknown site.

->

SCP-4885 is theorized to be contained within an unknown Class-17 Containment Chamber in an unknown site. In the event that SCP-4885 breaches containment…

SCP-4885-1 instances discovered by far2.AIC will have specialized Foundation carrier drones to be dispatched and attempt to grab the body, and carry it to Location i, using a specialized AI to take routes that minimize the chance of unwanted civilian interference.

->

SCP-4885-1 instances will be recovered by specialized Foundation carrier drones and relocated to Location i, using a specialized AI to minimize the chance of unwanted civilian interference.

In the event that Location i's whereabouts are discovered at large, the next personnel located within Document PAINT-THE-TOWN-YELLOW will establish the next Location i, and their immediate family will be notified of their death.

This is not a word choice gaffe, but it's worth nothing that the rest of the file doesn't explain what this document is.

This entails that SCP-4885 wears a horizontal red and white striped shirt, a red and white bobble hat, and jeans. However, a noticeable difference in appearance from the character is the entity's paler skin and the lack of eyes.

->

Like the non-anomalous character, SCP-4885 wears a horizontal red and white striped shirt, a red and white bobble hat, and jeans. In contrast to the character, the entity's skin is notably paler and its face lacks eyes.

M-2 begins to violently groan, all while holding their stomach.

->

M-2 begins to groan and hold their stomach.

+1 anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it the whole way through. Wonderfully written and beautifully executed. +2 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The whole piece is well conceived and written. The details of the containment were wonderfully specific. The description was very spooky and left me somewhat disturbed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't know how I managed to miss that. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good article, creepy +1

But, in the log where they found Waldo, I think you may have called M-2 M-3. It then says that -1 and -3 start firing, but the (presumed) typo refutes this.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: thank you for giving me an opportunity to draw something for your SCP!

item.title: One of my most confident +1s
item.content:encoded: I first saw this as a WAY earlier draft and was involved in a lot of crit, and even though it's vastly improved since than, it was always an incredible idea executed brilliantly. The containment procedures are creative and enticing, and the anomaly does what it does very well. 10/10 would get my eyes gored out by a cartoon character again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus. That was disturbing. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
image stuff
– hide block

The image was created by pastarasta1 and used with permission.

Edit: with huge thanks to Shaggydredlocks, the image has been updated to be more immersive. The background image is CC0 public domain.

Source.

WALDO FOUND MOMMY

Here's my attempt at a Scary Monster SCP™. Like I said, this month is full of firsts for me. Originally it was a lot more focused on the "waldo" part, but everyone said it didn't add anything, so this one is decently short.

Thanks to Henzoid, Popsioak, KindlyTurtleClem, Brewsterion 1017 and Yossipossi. I apologize if I forgot anyone else, I'm incredibly tired right now.

Art

He Has Found You
By DeadSpace123
Hello I am Waldo yes
by Henzoid
A collection of SCP art, including SCP-4885
by Mr-Firework
Waldo Found Mommy
by Dr. Gooday on the SCP Amino
Find Him
by CyanBlue983
An Unlucky Victim
by Vinevin

More of their excellent work can be found here.
